tweets.text:
This has been one of the most perplexing things I've experienced in crypto. I bull posted this onchain poker protocol several times leading up to its launch. 

And while there were some issues with MagicBlock for the first week which slowed down the hands tremendously these were fixed pretty quickly considering it's a mainnet beta. So I can't blame them. This is experimental tech after all.

Tokenomics also were reworked. I feel like this team (who I'm invested in through Gm Capital) we're on top of every little thing and really dialed in the gameplay to where it's super enjoyable poker. 

Many complained about the geo blocking. But I can't attribute lack of commercial success to that given that many crypto platforms geoblock and are successful. 

I can't blame Solana as they were very supportive and even posted about the protocol several times through the official gaming account. The timing was really perfect with WSOP and the sponsorship there.

I'm left just scratching my head wondering what in the world is happening. Is gaming really dead? Or was it never alive? Do people only care about mining a token and nobody really wanted to play something fun that actually works? 

It's pretty nihilist but it's really the only conclusion I can reach. Which also makes it tough as an investor in crypto on the venture side which I'm heavily involved in. 

Like how do you justify making these Investments when the execution is pretty close to perfect given it's a protocol built by one person essentially in their spare time and advertise as a mainnet beta so no surprises. Yet there were no catastrophic failures at all.

I don't really know where to go from here. I feel somewhat defeated. I would certainly expecting this to be a major commercial success. It still might be down the road. But for now I just take the L.

Also by limiting the node sales to the 585 that did sell already (half of those funds went to stakers) at the very least anybody who did buy is going to be in a much better position if this ever does take off. 

I certainly didn't want to leave anybody hanging and feel that that's the right move so I'm glad they implemented this.

Just wanted to get this off my chest for my followers here. As always I appreciate all the support and your willingness to pay attention to what I'm saying. I don't take that responsibility lightly.

tweets.text:
The UK extends the streak of softer-than-expected inflation data in advanced economies:
Headline CPI dropped to 2.6% in June from 2.8% in May, coming in below the 2.7% consensus forecast.
Core held steady at 2.6%, despite a further deceleration in service sector inflation from 3.7% to 3.6%.
The trend of favorable inflation surprises helps explain why global rate expectations have stayed well-anchored despite higher oil prices.
Even the ECB, widely viewed as the most hawkish of the major central banks, is broadly anticipated to hold rates steady, even if its accompanying guidance is hawkish.
#economy #markets #UK #inflation

I’ve been banging this drum for the past few years…

Agentic AI is expected to drive $3-5T of commerce by 2030, yet I still think most investors are looking in the wrong place

Crypto isn’t just another AI vertical, it becomes the financial infrastructure that allows autonomous AI agents to transact with one another

Everyone is positioning for AI through semis, hyperscalers & infrastructure, but that only captures the compute layer

As AI evolves from generatin content to executing transactions, agents will need to pay for compute, data, APIs and services autonomously

That requires instant settlement, negligible fees and programmable money, all things blockchain networks were designed to provide

If billions of machines are transacting every day, owning AI companies alone may not be enough, the networks and tokens facilitating those payments could capture a meaningful share of the value

The CLARITY Act could be the catalyst that brings institutional confidence to this thesis by providing regulatory clarity around digital assets while enabling more transparent token structures and stronger ownership rights for token holders

The next decade won’t be focused on owning AI; it’ll be about owning the networks that AI runs on

tweets.text:
The economy remains healthy, but rising leverage has left the market increasingly fragile.

With growth holding up and recession risk still low, our cyclical macro regime remains firmly risk-on. The fundamental backdrop still supports equities even as speculative positioning becomes more stretched.

Leveraged ETF assets have reached a record of roughly $218B after rising around 60% since the end of March. Retail options activity is also breaking records, with nearly half of retail options volume now concentrated in 0DTE contracts. Meanwhile, demand for downside protection has faded.

That combination makes the market vulnerable to a sharp volatility event. If momentum breaks, leveraged products would be forced to rebalance and crowded portfolios could begin cutting exposure. An ordinary pullback could become self reinforcing even if the outlook hasn't changed.

This is the volatility event we continue to expect in 2026. If growth holds and credit continues to flow, the selloff would clear excess leverage from an otherwise healthy cycle.

In recent days the CLARITY Act has gained renewed momentum post the compromise preventing senior officials benefitting from digital assets.

It looks like this version of the CLARITY Act as it stands will have a vote in the Senate before the August recess.

Post the Senate passing the CLARITY Act the House must then agree on the amended bill and then vote to pass the amended version. Concluding requires a presidential signature then implementation from the CFTC/SEC of the CLARITY Act.

If CLARITY passes and implemented under new SEC and CFTC leadership this will be the final piece of the regulatory puzzle when it comes to Institutional adoption and on-shoring of DeFi protocols.

My hopes this also attracts exceptional founders back into the Industry and revives venture capital in a major way.

Optimistic

My best guess is a bit more time and a bit more pain to see us through this bear market. 

If you look at this chart then you can see how mild we have had it so far.

One of the features of a Bitcoin bear is the optimism that this time will be different for *insert various reasons (I am human and also love to think about why things could be different). 

I think there are events/catalysts that cause markets to move in very unexpected ways, but I personally don't see that set up for Bitcoin right now. And find it unlikely that BTC would suddenly find itself in a hurry, and deviate dramatically from very strong historical cyclical behaviour. 

The macro also seems to support further BTC downwards drift eventually (AI trade done for now, sticky inflation, real rates rising etc).  

The good news is you can be forward looking now, and focus on being a buyer with history on your side for 27-28.
